senators.name: Rick Scott
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Missouri-Kansas City and J.D. from SMU. Navy radar technician. Built Columbia/HCA into the largest for-profit healthcare company. Business acumen and scale of operation show executive analytical skills, but approach to governance is more business-instinct than data-driven. Healthcare fraud settlement under his leadership raises questions about evidence-based decision-making.

senators.name: Ashley Moody
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. and M.S. in accounting from University of Florida, J.D. from UF Law, and LL.M. in international law from Stetson. Four post-secondary degrees across accounting, law, and international law. Became youngest judge in Florida at 31. Methodical legal and prosecutorial career demonstrates strong analytical rigor. Master's in accounting shows quantitative foundation.
education: LL.M. International Law, Stetson University College of Law
J.D., University of Florida School of Law

FL-01.name: Jimmy Patronis
FL-01.notNerdReason: A.A. in restaurant management from Gulf Coast Community College and B.A. in political science from Florida State University. Career in family restaurant business and state government (FL CFO). No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
education: B.A. Political Science, Florida State University
A.A. Restaurant Management, Gulf Coast Community College

FL-02.name: Neal Dunn
FL-02.nerdReason: B.S. in interdisciplinary sciences from Washington and Lee University and M.D. from George Washington University School of Medicine. Practiced as a urologist for over 25 years. U.S. Army surgeon (Major). Medical training and decades of clinical practice demonstrate deep scientific and analytical expertise applied to healthcare policy.
education: M.D., George Washington University School of Medicine
B.S. Interdisciplinary Sciences, Washington and Lee University

FL-03.name: Kat Cammack
FL-03.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science from Metropolitan State University of Denver. Career as congressional staffer (deputy chief of staff to Rep. Ted Yoho). Grew up on a cattle ranch. No analytical or technical background beyond political operations.
education: B.A. Political Science, Metropolitan State University of Denver
experience: Deputy Chief of Staff to Rep. Ted Yoho

FL-04.name: Aaron Bean
FL-04.notNerdReason: B.S. from Jacksonville University. Career in state legislature and family business. No notable analytical, technical, or evidence-based policy approach.

FL-05.name: John Rutherford
FL-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from Florida State University, graduate of FBI National Academy. 12 years as Jacksonville Sheriff. Law enforcement leadership requires data-driven crime reduction strategies, but overall approach is more operational than analytically rigorous.

FL-06.name: Randy Fine
FL-06.nerdReason: B.A. in government from Harvard University and M.B.A. from Harvard Business School. Elite academic credentials demonstrate strong analytical and quantitative foundation. Business background in healthcare and technology before entering politics. Harvard Business School training emphasizes data-driven decision-making.

FL-07.name: Cory Mills
FL-07.notNerdReason: U.S. Army combat veteran and defense contractor. No publicly documented college degree. Career in military and private defense sector. Operational and tactical expertise but no analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
education: U.S. Army training and military education

FL-08.name: Mike Haridopolos
FL-08.nerdReason: B.A. in history from Stetson University, M.A. in history from University of Arkansas, and Ph.D. from University of Arkansas. A doctorate in any field demonstrates deep commitment to research, analytical methodology, and scholarly rigor. Career as a college professor of U.S. history and political science before entering politics. Former president of the Florida State Senate.

FL-09.name: Darren Soto
FL-09.nerdReason: B.A. from Rutgers University and J.D. from George Washington University Law School. Career as attorney and state legislator with strong focus on technology, energy, and space policy. Serves on the Energy and Commerce Committee. Known for policy depth on blockchain, clean energy, and NASA-related issues.
education: J.D., George Washington University Law School

FL-10.name: Maxwell Frost
FL-10.notNerdReason: Attended Valencia College. Youngest member of Congress (Gen Z). Career in activism and political organizing (March for Our Lives, ACLU). Passionate advocate but approach is driven by grassroots activism rather than analytical or research-based frameworks.
experience: National Organizing Director, March for Our Lives

FL-11.name: Daniel Webster
FL-11.nerdReason: B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Georgia Institute of Technology. One of very few members of Congress with an engineering degree. Career in air conditioning and heating business before entering politics, applying technical knowledge practically. Rose to Speaker of the Florida House. Engineering background provides genuine analytical and problem-solving foundation.
education: B.S. Electrical Engineering, Georgia Institute of Technology

FL-12.name: Gus Bilirakis
FL-12.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Florida and J.D. from Stetson University College of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Serves on Energy and Commerce Committee with focus on health policy. Solid policy engagement but primarily legal and political background without deep analytical rigor.
education: J.D., Stetson University College of Law
experience: Son of former Rep. Michael Bilirakis

FL-13.name: Anna Paulina Luna
FL-13.notNerdReason: B.A. in biology from University of West Florida. Air Force veteran. Biology degree provides some STEM foundation but limited evidence of applying scientific background in legislative work. Policy focus driven by ideology rather than evidence-based analysis.
education: B.A. Biology, University of West Florida

FL-14.name: Kathy Castor
FL-14.nerdReason: B.A. from Emory University and J.D. from Florida State University. Career as attorney focused on environmental and consumer protection. Chaired the House Select Committee on the Climate Crisis, producing a detailed, data-driven climate action plan. Deep policy expertise in climate and energy backed by research and analysis.
education: J.D., Florida State University College of Law
experience: Former Chair, House Select Committee on Climate Crisis

FL-15.name: Laurel Lee
FL-15.nerdReason: B.A. from University of Florida and J.D. from University of Florida Levin College of Law. Federal prosecutor and circuit court judge. Served as Florida Secretary of State overseeing election security and cybersecurity initiatives. Legal career demonstrates strong analytical methodology. Election administration role required engagement with data systems and technology.
education: J.D., University of Florida Levin College of Law

FL-16.name: Vern Buchanan
FL-16.notNerdReason: B.B.A. from Cleary University and M.B.A. from University of Detroit. Successful businessman (auto dealerships, Ritz-Carlton franchise). Business education and entrepreneurial success show practical analytical skills but governance approach is more business-oriented than research-driven.
experience: Chairman, House Ways and Means Committee

FL-17.name: Greg Steube
FL-17.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Florida and J.D. from University of Florida Levin College of Law. U.S. Army veteran (JAG officer, Iraq). Legal and military background. Policy positions driven by ideology rather than data or evidence-based frameworks.
education: J.D., University of Florida Levin College of Law

FL-18.name: Scott Franklin
FL-18.nerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Naval Academy and M.B.A. from Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University. 26-year career as a Navy pilot and officer. Naval Academy education provides rigorous technical and engineering foundation. Embry-Riddle is a leading aerospace institution. Military aviation requires exceptional analytical skills, precision, and data-driven decision-making.

FL-19.name: Byron Donalds
FL-19.notNerdReason: B.S. in finance from Florida State University. Career in banking, finance, and insurance. Financial background provides quantitative foundation but legislative approach is more ideologically driven than analytically rigorous.

FL-20.name: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick
FL-20.notNerdReason: B.A. from Howard University and J.D. from St. Thomas University School of Law. CEO of a healthcare company. Legal and healthcare management background shows business analytical skills but not distinctly research-driven or evidence-based governance.
education: J.D., St. Thomas University School of Law

FL-21.name: Brian Mast
FL-21.nerdReason: B.A. in economics from Harvard Extension School. U.S. Army explosive ordnance disposal technician — one of the most technically demanding military specialties, requiring deep analytical and scientific knowledge. Lost both legs in Afghanistan clearing IEDs. Post-injury, worked as an explosive specialist for DHS and NNSA. Purple Heart and Bronze Star recipient. Combines technical military expertise with analytical economics education.
education: B.A. Economics, Harvard University Extension School
experience: U.S. Army, Explosive Ordnance Disposal Technician
Explosive Specialist, Dept. of Homeland Security
Purple Heart and Bronze Star recipient

FL-22.name: Lois Frankel
FL-22.nerdReason: B.A. from Boston University and J.D. from Georgetown University Law Center. Career as attorney, state legislator, and mayor of West Palm Beach. Georgetown Law graduate. Long legislative track record with deep policy expertise in women's rights and foreign affairs. Methodical approach to policy.

FL-23.name: Jared Moskowitz
FL-23.nerdReason: B.A. from George Washington University and J.D. from Nova Southeastern University. Served as director of the Florida Division of Emergency Management, managing multi-billion dollar disaster response operations requiring data-driven logistics and analytical planning. Deep expertise in crisis management, public health response, and FEMA coordination demonstrates analytical rigor applied to governance.
experience: Director, Florida Division of Emergency Management

FL-24.name: Frederica Wilson
FL-24.notNerdReason: B.S. from Fisk University and M.S. from University of Miami. Career as educator and school administrator. Education policy expertise built on practical experience rather than research-driven approach. Deep commitment to education but not a distinctly analytical policy voice.

FL-25.name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
FL-25.nerdReason: B.A. and M.A. in political science from University of Florida. Former chair of the Democratic National Committee. Deep policy expertise across healthcare, education, and appropriations built over decades in Congress. Known for meticulous organizational and strategic approach. Serves on Appropriations Committee with detailed oversight of federal spending.
education: M.A. Political Science, University of Florida
B.A. Political Science, University of Florida

FL-26.name: Mario Diaz-Balart
FL-26.notNerdReason: Attended University of South Florida but did not graduate. Career entirely in state and federal politics. Deep experience in government but no academic credentials or analytical policy background. Long-tenured appropriator with practical political skills.
education: University of South Florida (attended, no degree)

FL-27.name: Maria Elvira Salazar
FL-27.notNerdReason: B.A. in communications from University of Miami. Career as Emmy Award-winning TV journalist. Strong communication skills and international affairs knowledge but approach is more narrative-driven than analytical. No technical or research background.
experience: TV Journalist, Univision and Telemundo (Emmy Award-winning)

FL-28.name: Carlos Gimenez
FL-28.notNerdReason: B.A. from Barry University and M.P.A. from Florida International University. Career as firefighter rising to fire chief, then mayor of Miami-Dade County (largest county in FL). MPA provides public administration analytical skills. Emergency management and county leadership require data-informed decisions but overall approach is more operational than research-driven.
